repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Problem: No public method for treesitter incremental selection Solution: Add a method `vim.treesitter.select()` Closes: #38211
**TODO** - [x] 1. ~~Return information about the selection? (There is a comment where I can return coordinates)~~ Sounds like it can be done later - [x] 2. ~~Generated docs and news~~

comment_data.body:
the name `select` is unfortunate since it normally refers to selecting a menu item. an existing precedent is lsp `selection_range` but LSP names don't generally leak into the rest of Nvim if we have other conventions.

WDYT about naming it `visual()` @echasnovski ?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -508,4 +509,39 @@ function M.foldexpr(lnum)
   return M._fold.foldexpr(lnum)
 end
 
+--- @class vim.treesitter.select.Opts
+--- @inlinedoc
+---
+--- Direction to select in
+--- @field direction 'parent'|'child'|'next'|'prev'|'grow_next'|'grow_prev'
+---
+--- Number of selections to make in the given direction (default 1)
+--- @field count integer?
+
+--- Perform an incremental selection based on the given direction
+---@param opts vim.treesitter.select.Opts
+function M.select(opts)

@@ -508,4 +509,39 @@ function M.foldexpr(lnum)
   return M._fold.foldexpr(lnum)
 end
 
+--- @class vim.treesitter.select.Opts
+--- @inlinedoc
+---
+--- Direction to select in
+--- @field direction 'parent'|'child'|'next'|'prev'|'grow_next'|'grow_prev'
+---
+--- Number of selections to make in the given direction (default 1)
+--- @field count integer?
+
+--- Perform an incremental selection based on the given direction
+---@param opts vim.treesitter.select.Opts
+function M.select(opts)
+  vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table')
+  vim.validate('count', opts.count, 'number', true)

@@ -508,4 +509,39 @@ function M.foldexpr(lnum)
   return M._fold.foldexpr(lnum)
 end
 
+--- @class vim.treesitter.select.Opts
+--- @inlinedoc
+---
+--- Direction to select in
+--- @field direction 'parent'|'child'|'next'|'prev'|'grow_next'|'grow_prev'
+---
+--- Number of selections to make in the given direction (default 1)
+--- @field count integer?
+
+--- Perform an incremental selection based on the given direction
+---@param opts vim.treesitter.select.Opts
+function M.select(opts)
+  vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table')
+  vim.validate('count', opts.count, 'number', true)
+
+  local direction = opts.direction
+  local count = opts.count or 1
+
+  if direction == 'parent' then
+    return M._select.select_parent(count)
+  elseif direction == 'child' then
+    return M._select.select_child(count)
+  elseif direction == 'next' then
+    return M._select.select_next(count)
+  elseif direction == 'prev' then
+    return M._select.select_prev(count)
+  elseif direction == 'grow_next' then
+    return M._select.select_grow_next(count)
+  elseif direction == 'grow_prev' then
+    return M._select.select_grow_prev(count)
+  else
+    error("Invalid value for option 'direction'")

comment_data.body:
> WDYT about naming it `visual()` @echasnovski ?

I think it at least should be a verb. To me, reading `vim.treesitter.visual()` would be ambiguous at what it does. The first guess would be that it returns the data about the visual selection somehow related to tree-sitter.

> the name `select` is unfortunate since it normally refers to selecting a menu item. an existing precedent is lsp `selection_range` but LSP names don't generally leak into the rest of Nvim if we have other conventions.

Well, the `:h Visual` has this intro:

> Visual mode is a flexible and easy way to select a piece of text for an operator.  It is the only way to select a block of text.

Which uses "select" twice to mean "select visually". I think it using plain `vim.treesitter.select()` is fine. If not, then maybe more verbosity might help? Like `select_node()`, `select_range()`, `select_visual()`?

comment_data.body: I don't have strong opinion, but I'd guess making it take and use `direction` argument directly would make tests clearer. This would make this test file about `vim.treesitter.select()` (rather than private `require('vim.treesitter._select')`). This would require replacing all calls to use different enum values, though, and thus a big diff.

comment_data.body:
sorry I didn't notice this before: `direction` should be arg1, because:

- it's required
- improves readability (`select('foo')` vs `select({direction='foo'})`
- lets `direction` be a positional param, which keeps the door open to improve its name later ( `direction=child/next/etc` is a bit weird)

```suggestion
function M.select(direction, opts)
```

comment_data.body: I guess vim.validate isn't gaining much here, so my suggestion was off. I'll tweak this in the "docs" PR.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -508,4 +509,42 @@ function M.foldexpr(lnum)
   return M._fold.foldexpr(lnum)
 end
 
+--- @class vim.treesitter.select.Opts
+--- @inlinedoc
+---
+--- Number of selections to make in the given direction (default 1)
+--- @field count integer?
+
+--- Starts or adjusts a |Visual| selection at cursor, based on tree nodes.
+---@param direction 'parent'|'child'|'next'|'prev'|'extend_next'|'extend_prev' Direction to select
+---                                                                            towards
+---@param opts vim.treesitter.select.Opts?
+function M.select(direction, opts)
+  vim.validate('direction', direction, 'string')
+  vim.validate('opts', opts, 'table', true)
+  opts = opts or {}
+  if opts.count then
+    vim.validate('count', opts.count, 'number')
+  end
+  local count = opts.count or 1
+
+  if direction == 'parent' then
+    return M._select.select_parent(count)
+  elseif direction == 'child' then
+    return M._select.select_child(count)
+  elseif direction == 'next' then
+    return M._select.select_next(count)
+  elseif direction == 'prev' then
+    return M._select.select_prev(count)
+  elseif direction == 'extend_next' then
+    return M._select.select_grow_next(count)
+  elseif direction == 'extend_prev' then
+    return M._select.select_grow_prev(count)
+  else
+    vim.validate('direction', direction, function()
+      return false, 'Invalid direction'
+    end)

comment_regular_data.body:
Would it be prohibitively difficult for this to return info about the selection and/or info about the selected node? This would make it possible to take the selection and build functionality on top of it.

Use case: Plugins like treeclimber or flash, which show sibling node info on selection.
@mikejmcguirk Probably not, depends on what information is wanted I guess? I added a comment below where you can get the coordinates of the selection pretty easily.

> @mikejmcguirk Probably not, depends on what information is wanted I guess? I added a comment below where you can get the coordinates of the selection pretty easily.

To me this makes sense. It's enough to experiment with, but avoids major code changes based on what might end up being speculation.

EDIT: One issue could be - If this change is backported, that implies a level of commitment about what the public function returns. If this isn't the right place to make that decision, then I'd be fine making a follow-up issue to think through it more thoroughly.

@justinmk, are there any concerns with the API? To me the code change looks like good to merge (the generated docs and news are left).

This is a fairly important follow up to the `v_an` and `v_in` keymaps, as it allows to cleanly use other keys for the functionality *and* decide to prefer either tree-sitter or LSP. I've tested this with the following config and it works as expected:

```lua
vim.keymap.set({ 'x', 'o' }, 'grs', function()
  if vim.treesitter.get_parser(nil, nil, { error = false }) then
    require('vim.treesitter').select({ direction = 'parent', count = vim.v.count1 })
  else
    vim.lsp.buf.selection_range(vim.v.count1)
  end
end, { desc = 'Select parent (outer) node' })

vim.keymap.set({ 'x', 'o' }, 'grS', function()
  if vim.treesitter.get_parser(nil, nil, { error = false }) then
    require('vim.treesitter').select({ direction = 'child', count = vim.v.count1 })
  else
    vim.lsp.buf.selection_range(-vim.v.count1)
  end
end, { desc = 'Select child (inner) node' })
```

I added `'grow_next'` and `'grow_prev'` as directions, since that was merged since the creation of the PR.

I also updated the PR description with todo tasks. If we decide to move this forward, I may need some help / time to figure out the docs and news as I haven't done it before.

> I also updated the PR description with todo tasks. If we decide to move this forward, I may need some help / time to figure out the docs and news as I haven't done it before.

I would mark this as "ready for review" and wait for the review. If this is accepted, I can help with docs and news. But there doesn't seem to be a lot missing:
- Run locally `make doc` to generate documentation.
- Add an entry to a corresponding news file if needed. It is [`news.txt`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/runtime/doc/news.txt) if this won't be backported to 0.12.3. If it is backported, then either no news or [`news-0.12.txt`](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/runtime/doc/news-0.12.txt) (I am not sure what is the preferred approach here).

LGTM, for test coverage I would suggest updating https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/7e813c65f7007d0e72d8ab894ba7999037a0add4/test/functional/treesitter/select_spec.lua#L26-L32

to call this interface, rather than adding duplicate tests.
Comments have been addressed. `news.txt` has not been updated yet, as it sounds like we weren't sure where it was going to go.

> `news.txt` has not been updated yet, as it sounds like we weren't sure where it was going to go.

`news-features`

> > `news.txt` has not been updated yet, as it sounds like we weren't sure where it was going to go.
> 
> `news-features`

Do you plan this to be backported to 0.12.3? If yes, then is it a good idea to update `news-0.12.txt`?

> If yes, then is it a good idea to update `news-0.12.txt`?

No, the current pattern is to update news.txt in both the master and `release-0-.12` branches. There are arguments for other approaches but all have tradeoffs, let's keep doing it the way we are currently doing it until we have a better approach: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/39608
Comments addressed, `news.txt` has been updated. Let me know what you think.

Backport failed for `release-0.12`, because it was unable to cherry-pick the commit(s).

Please cherry-pick the changes locally and resolve any conflicts.
```bash
git fetch origin release-0.12
git worktree add -d .worktree/backport-39069-to-release-0.12 origin/release-0.12
cd .worktree/backport-39069-to-release-0.12
git switch --create backport-39069-to-release-0.12
git cherry-pick -x c286c9d686cbe7b9152c1b87bb241bdb30b4b21f
```
